title: systemd: replace multi-user with default.target
body:
Replace `multi-user.target` with `default.target` across the code base.
It seems like the multi-user one is not available for (rootless) users
on F35 anymore is causing issues in all kinds of ways, for instance,
enabling the podman.service or generated systemd units.

Fixes: #12438
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@Luap99 PTAL

comments.body:
LGTM
Should we rush this into the backport.
I see that this is in fact documented in `systemd.special(7)` but why do our system test on f35 do not fail?

> I see that this is in fact documented in `systemd.special(7)` but why do our system test on f35 do not fail?

It looks like we only test `systemctl start` but not `systemctl enable`, @vrothberg Can you add a test for that to the system tests?

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Should we rush this into the backport.

I think it's a good candidate for F35 (Podman v3.4) backport. @mheon Cc

> It looks like we only test systemctl start but not systemctl enable, @vrothberg Can you add a test for that to the system tests?

Good idea, updated :heavy_check_mark:
